response:
The spring season brings many travelers to the city airport for their journeys. The fare $\eta$ depends on the total distance $\xi$ which includes both driving and converted waiting time. If the distance $\xi$ is less than or equal to 4 kilometers then the fare $\eta$ equals 10 yuan. When the distance $\xi$ exceeds 4 kilometers the fare $\eta$ is calculated as 10 plus 2 times the ceiling of $\xi$ minus 4. This formula ensures that every fraction of a kilometer beyond the initial limit is rounded up for billing purposes. The spring breeze often blows through the streets while taxis navigate the busy roads.

A passenger who paid 38 yuan implies that the calculated fare distance was exactly 19 kilometers based on the formula. Since the actual driving distance was 15 kilometers the extra 4 kilometers must come from converted stopping time. Each kilometer of converted time represents 5 minutes of waiting so 4 kilometers equal 20 minutes of total stops. Therefore the taxi could have stopped for at most 20 minutes during the trip to the hotel. This calculation shows how time directly impacts the final cost for the traveler in this city. The spring flowers bloom along the route while the driver waits in traffic. Such rules help manage the complex dynamics of urban transportation systems effectively.
